description: I first noticed the bird when we flushed a small group of meadowlarks as we drove slowly along the dirt road from JM State Park and SWA. When it landed in front of vehicle (on my side) it showed far more white than its companions or what one might expect in a WEME (more than 2/3 of spread tail). After landing, the bird stood rather upright on ground about 15-20 feet away and was easily visible. The cheek was bright white including the malar, with absolultely no intrusion of yellow into the white malar. Crown striped brown and white, showing more contrast than nearby WEMEs. The line behind the eye was quite blackish and the supercilium white, like the cheek. The throat was yellow. The wing coverts and tertials were barred like WEME. Later I could see yellow on chest and belly with the black chest V of a meadowlark. The sides white with blackish markings, but I did not look closely here (so, can't say streaks, spots, black, brown, etc). After 30 or so seconds, the bird flew landing about 25 feet away facing largely away. The brown central tail feathers were barred but lacked dark longitudinal markings (ie, parallel to shaft of feathers). The uppertail coverts were striped like EAME (that is, had dark along shaft of feathers with some dark cross markings. Buzzy zhit call heard twice at close range. Dave and I gave pursuit, flushing the bird several more times; again, the extra white on tail was quite clear, when we did flush it, but we wer unable to relocate bird on ground. Note description was written within 5 minutes of the bird last being seen
